additionalTime: 10 minutes quick soak plus 10 minutes coating rest
author: Robert Carr
description: A quick hot honey chicken cutlet sandwich with a thin chicken breast cutlet, a buttermilk-hot sauce dip, crisp panko crust, garlic mayo, lime slaw, dill pickles, and a toasted potato bun.
equipment: Cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan

groceryIngredients.item: plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix
groceryIngredients.display: plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix

ingredients: 1 boneless skinless chicken breast cutlet, about 6 ounces
1 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or 1/2 teaspoon Morton kosher salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
1 tablespoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, such as Frank's RedHot, Crystal, or Texas Pete, divided
1 (16-ounce) jar dill pickle chips, using 1/4 cup pickle chips and 2 tablespoons pickle brine
3/4 cup avocado oil or another neutral high-heat oil, or enough for a 1/4-inch layer
3/4 cup plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix
instructions: Set 1 wire rack inside a rimmed baking sheet for the breaded raw chicken, then set a second clean wire rack inside another rimmed baking sheet for the fried chicken.
If the chicken breast is not already a thin cutlet, split it horizontally and save the extra cutlet for another meal.
Place the chicken cutlet between sheets of plastic wrap or parchment and pound it to an even 1/2 inch thickness.
Season both sides of the chicken cutlet with 1/4 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together 1/4 cup buttermilk, 1 large egg, 1 teaspoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, and 1 teaspoon pickle brine from the dill pickle jar.
Add the seasoned chicken cutlet to the buttermilk mixture and turn until fully coated.
Let the chicken sit in the buttermilk mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes while making the sauce and slaw.

Make the garlic mayo by stirring together 2 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/2 teaspoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1/2 teaspoon honey, a pinch smoked paprika, a pinch garlic powder, and a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt.
Refrigerate the garlic mayo until assembly.

Make the lime slaw by whisking together 1 tablespoon mayonnaise, 1 teaspoon fresh lime juice, 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar, 1 teaspoon pickle brine, 1/2 teaspoon honey, a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a pinch black pepper in a medium bowl.
Add 3/4 cup plain shredded cabbage and carrot coleslaw mix and the thinly sliced green onion to the lime dressing.
Toss the slaw for 30 seconds, until all of the cabbage is lightly coated, then refrigerate it until assembly.

Make the dry coating in a wide shallow bowl by whisking together 1/4 cup all-purpose flour, 1/3 cup panko breadcrumbs, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 1/4 teaspoon baking powder, 1/4 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/4 teaspoon onion powder, 1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper, 1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and a pinch MSG if using.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon of the buttermilk mixture into the dry coating and rub it through with your fingers until small shaggy clumps form.
Remove 1 chicken cutlet from the buttermilk mixture and let the excess drip back into the bowl for 3 to 5 seconds.
Press the chicken cutlet firmly into the dry coating on both sides.
Dip the coated chicken cutlet back into the buttermilk mixture for 1 second, then let the excess drip off.
Press the chicken cutlet into the dry coating a second time, packing the panko-flour mixture onto both sides until there are no wet spots.
Place the breaded chicken cutlet on the raw-chicken wire rack.
Let the breaded chicken rest on the raw-chicken wire rack for 10 minutes so the coating absorbs moisture and sticks.
While the chicken rests, spread 1/2 tablespoon softened unsalted butter over the cut sides of the potato bun.
Heat the cast-iron skillet or heavy saute pan over medium heat, then toast the buttered bun cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes, until golden brown.
Move the toasted bun to a plate and wipe out any loose crumbs from the skillet.
Pour 3/4 cup avocado oil into the skillet, or enough to make a 1/4-inch layer.
Heat the oil over medium to medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees F.
Fry the chicken cutlet without crowding the pan.
Cook the first side for 3 to 4 minutes, until deep golden brown and crisp.
Flip the chicken cutlet with tongs and cook the second side for 2 to 3 minutes, until deep golden brown and the center reaches 165 degrees F.
Transfer the fried chicken cutlet to the clean wire rack so steam can escape.

Make the hot honey butter by warming the remaining 1/2 tablespoon unsalted butter, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 teaspoon Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce, 1/2 teaspoon apple cider vinegar, the remaining 1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper, and a tiny pinch Diamond Crystal kosher salt in a small saucepan or microwave-safe bowl until the butter melts.
Stir the hot honey butter until glossy and smooth.
Brush or spoon the hot honey butter over both sides of the fried chicken cutlet while it is still hot.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on the toasted bottom bun.
Add 1/4 cup dill pickle chips to the bottom bun.
Place the hot honey chicken cutlet on the bottom bun.
Top the chicken cutlet with about 1/2 cup lime slaw.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic mayo on the toasted top bun.
Close the sandwich and serve immediately while the chicken crust is crisp.
notes: Use a thin Louisiana-style cayenne hot sauce. It should taste salty, vinegary, and peppery, not smoky or sweet.
Buy dill pickle chips, not sweet bread-and-butter pickles, because the brine seasons the chicken dip, garlic mayo, and slaw.
Pounding the chicken to 1/2 inch matters because the coating browns quickly; thick chicken can overcook on the outside before the center reaches 165 degrees F.
The 10-minute coating rest helps the dry flour patches hydrate so the crust clings in the skillet.
Use separate racks for raw breaded chicken and fried chicken. The clean rack also keeps the bottom crust from steaming soft.
Keep the oil between 325 degrees F and 350 degrees F while frying. If the coating browns before the chicken reaches 165 degrees F, lower the heat slightly.
The slaw should stay lightly dressed, not wet. If the coleslaw mix releases liquid while waiting, lift the slaw with tongs and leave extra dressing behind during assembly.
For a less spicy sandwich, skip the cayenne pepper in the dry coating and hot honey butter.
For a hotter sandwich, add 1/8 to 1/4 teaspoon extra cayenne pepper to the hot honey butter.
Serve right after brushing with hot honey butter so the crust stays crisp and the bun stays toasted.
title: Hot Honey Chicken Cutlet Sandwich
